topic: Food, Drink & Eating Out: Meals, Tastes & Partitives

subUnits.title: 4.1 Meals & Taste Adjectives
notes: - Adjectives agree in gender with the food noun.
- La mangue est sucrée. (The mango is sweet.)
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French meals vocabulary tastes adjectives site:youtube.com

quiz.q: How do you say 'breakfast'?
What is 'lunch' in French?
Which word means 'dinner'?
How do you say 'afternoon snack'?
What does 'délicieux' mean?
Which word means 'sweet'?
How do you say 'salty'?
What is 'spicy' in French?
Which word means 'bitter'?
How do you say 'The soup is salty'?

subUnits.title: 4.2 Partitive Articles (Some/Any)
notes: - de la (feminine singular): de la viande (some meat)
- de l' (before vowel): de l'eau (some water)
- Je mange du riz. (I eat some rice.)
- Elle boit de l'eau. (She drinks some water.)
- Nous achetons des fruits. (We buy some fruits.)
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French partitive articles du de la des site:youtube.com

quiz.q: Which article is used for 'some bread' (pain - m)?
What is 'some meat' (viande - f)?
How do you say 'some water'?
quiz.explanation: 'eau' starts with a vowel, so 'de l''.

quiz.q: Which article is used for 'some fruits'?
How do you say 'I eat some rice'?
What is 'She drinks some milk'?
How do you say 'We buy some tomatoes'?
Which is correct for 'some tea' (thé - m)?
What is 'some soup' (soupe - f)?
How do you say 'He wants some fish'?

subUnits.title: 4.3 Negation with Partitives & Restaurant Dialogue
notes: - In negative sentences, ALL partitives (du, de la, de l', des) become 'de' or 'd''.
- Je mange du pain. -> Je ne mange pas de pain.
- Je bois de l'eau. -> Je ne bois pas d'eau.
- Une table pour deux, s'il vous plaît. (A table for two, please.)
- Je voudrais du jus d'orange. (I would like some orange juice.)
- L'addition, s'il vous plaît! (The bill, please!)
subUnits.youtubeQuery: JSS3 French negation partitives restaurant dialogue site:youtube.com

quiz.q: What does 'du' become in a negative sentence?
How do you say 'I do not eat bread'?
opts: Je ne mange pas du pain
Je ne mange pas de pain

quiz.q: What is 'She does not drink water'?
opts: Elle ne boit pas de l'eau

quiz.q: How do you ask for a table for two?
opts: Une table pour deux, s'il vous plaît
quiz.explanation: 'Une table pour deux, s'il vous plaît' is the standard phrase.

quiz.q: What does 'Je voudrais du jus' mean?
quiz.explanation: 'Je voudrais' is the polite conditional 'I would like'.

quiz.q: How do you ask for the bill?
What does 'C'est délicieux' mean?
How do you say 'We do not buy fruits'?
What is 'Thank you, sir'?
How do you say 'I am full' (simple)?
quiz.explanation: Saying 'It was delicious, thank you' is a polite way to indicate you are finished.
